message:
test(e2e): comprehensive keyboard and overlay system regression tests

Adds three new Playwright E2E test suites to prevent regressions in the
keyboard/overlay systems:

command-palette-drift.e2e.ts:
- Verifies CommandPalette shortcut hints match ActionRegistry definitions
- Tests multiple actions (Settings, Help, GC Skew, etc.) for shortcut accuracy
- Catches "drift" where UI shortcuts diverge from ActionRegistry source
- Uses setupTestHarness for structured logging and artifact capture
- Platform-aware assertions (expects ⌘ on macOS, Ctrl on others)

hotkeys-stack.e2e.ts:
- Tests overlay stack limit enforcement (default 3)
- Opens 4 overlays in sequence: Help → Settings → CommandPalette → AnalysisMenu
- Verifies oldest overlay (Help) is evicted when stack exceeds limit
- Tests toast notification appears on eviction
- Tests undo functionality (Ctrl+Z restores evicted overlay)
- Validates stack count remains at limit after eviction

overlay-lazy-loading.e2e.ts:
- Verifies no overlay chunks load during initial page load
- Monitors network requests for overlay-specific JavaScript chunks
- Opens HelpOverlay and verifies chunk loads on-demand
- Measures time from interaction to chunk request
- Ensures lazy loading is working correctly (not eager)
- Validates that core app bundle doesn't include overlay code

All tests use the shared e2e-harness for:
- Structured console/network logging
- Artifact capture on failure
- Consistent test setup patterns

These tests guard against:
1. Shortcut display diverging from ActionRegistry
2. Overlay stack logic breaking (limit, eviction, undo)
3. Lazy loading regressing to eager loading (bundle bloat)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
